description: Section 154 allows rectification of a mistake apparent from the record in an income tax order (143(1) intimation or 143(3) assessment order). For taxpayer-initiated errors in the original return, use revised return u/s 139(5) instead. Rectification u/s 154 addresses errors in the ORDER, not the return.
timeline: Application for rectification: within 4 years from end of AY in which order passed. AO must pass rectification order within 6 months of application.
whoCanApply: Taxpayer (assessee): If AO made an error in the assessment order
Assessing Officer: Can rectify suo motu (on their own motion) within 4 years
CPC: For errors in intimation u/s 143(1)
commonRectificationGrounds: TDS credit not given despite appearing in Form 26AS
Double counting of income in assessment order

steps.description: Review 143(1) intimation or assessment order. Note specific errors. Error must be 'apparent from record' — cannot introduce new claims or arguments not in original return.
E-filing portal → Services → Rectification Request. Select AY and order type. Describe the error and attach supporting documents (Form 26AS, 16, 16A).
AO reviews application and passes rectification order within 6 months. If rectification allowed: new intimation issued with corrected tax/refund. If rejected: reasons communicated.
If AO refuses rectification unfairly, appeal to CIT(A) u/s 246A within 30 days of rectification refusal order.

distinction.section154: Rectify errors in AO's ORDER. Cannot change what was filed in return.
distinction.section139(5): Revise what was filed in the original RETURN (taxpayer's own errors). Before March 31, 2027.
distinction.section139(8A): Updated return — can be filed within 2 years. Pay additional tax (25%-50% extra). Cannot reduce income.

commonMistakes: Filing rectification to introduce new deductions not claimed in original return (not allowed — must file revised return)
Not attaching supporting documents — rectification rejected without evidence
consequences: If rectification approved: Refund issued or demand reduced
If ignored and demand exists: Recovery proceedings u/s 220
